rationale: clauses: SOP addresses every checklist item(KILLED: removing [[P11:11.10i]] from the SOP -> audit_for_gaps!=0; KILLED: removing [[P11:11.10e]] likewise), gap analysis reports full coverage and is falsifiable(KILLED: deleting the P11:11.10e line from the checklist makes the stripped-SOP arm return 0 not 3, failing the ==3 assertion - proves the audit genuinely detects gaps rather than vacuously returning 0). SURVIVED (documented, equivalent): deleting a different checklist line (P11:11.100) passes - shrinking the checklist does not violate the DI, whose direction is SOP-coverage-of-the-checklist, not checklist immutability.
